fields.Identifying_Data_Chief_Complaint: 27-year-old Asian American female with schizophrenia for medication check.
fields.Subjective: Patient presented appearing well-groomed and engaged. Reports consistent medication adherence, taking risperidone 6 mg nightly using phone alarm. States doing quite well. Experiences occasional brief whispers once or twice weekly which she easily dismisses. No other hallucinations or paranoid thoughts. Sleep good at 7 hours nightly. Appetite healthy, weight stable at 128 lbs. Working part-time as customer service representative at call center, managing well. Mood 'good' rated 7/10. Lives with parents which provides support and cultural connection. Attends Asian American young adult support group monthly. Pursuing online college courses toward business degree. Enjoys K-pop music and watching Korean dramas. One year clean from methamphetamine. Good relationship with family. No safety concerns, denies SI/HI. (Seen by Dr. Amy Zhang, MD, Psychiatrist)

Mental_Status_Examination.Appearance: Casually dressed, good grooming, calm presentation

Laboratory_Data.Recent_Lab_Results: CBC and CMP within normal limits.

fields.Psychosocial_Context: Lives with parents; working part-time; pursuing degree; one year clean; attending support group.
Assessment_and_Plan: Excellent medication adherence and symptom control.
fields.Author: Note written by Dr. Amy Zhang, MD, Psychiatrist — Pacific Mental Health Services, 2890 Pacific Drive, Los Angeles, CA 90012, Phone: (555) 338-7621, Fax: (555) 338-7622
